untranslatable_reason: Signal is primarily an options strategy (diagonal spread for credit) requiring multi-leg position management, expiration date tracking, strike price relationships, and premium comparisons. These concepts cannot be expressed using only price-based technical indicators. The core logic—'profits from short call ≥ cost of long call' and 'rally substantially after near-term expiration'—requires options-specific metrics (implied volatility, time decay, strike deltas, premium differential) not available in the allowed indicator set.
translation_notes: This is an advanced options income strategy rather than a directional price-based signal. A backtest engine using only price/volume/technical indicators cannot properly model diagonal spreads, rolling positions, or premium-based entry/exit logic. The signal would require an options-aware backtester with Greeks calculation, volatility surfaces, and multi-leg position tracking.
